examples.title: Full Adder Behavior
examples.description: Behavioral representation of full adder operation
examples.explanation: This pseudocode describes the behavior of a full adder which adds three binary bits and produces sum and carry outputs using XOR and AND/OR gates.

examples.title: 4-bit Ripple Carry Adder Simulation
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating ripple carry addition with carry propagation
examples.code:
def half_adder(a, b):
    '''Half adder: adds two bits'''
    sum_bit = a ^ b
    carry = a & b
    return sum_bit, carry

def full_adder(a, b, cin):
    '''Full adder: adds three bits'''
    # First stage: add a and b
    s1, c1 = half_adder(a, b)
    # Second stage: add result to cin
    sum_bit, c2 = half_adder(s1, cin)
    # Carry is OR of both stage carries
    carry_out = c1 | c2
    return sum_bit, carry_out

def ripple_carry_adder(A, B):
    '''Add two 4-bit numbers using ripple carry'''
    result = 0
    carry = 0
    
    for i in range(4):
        a_bit = (A >> i) & 1
        b_bit = (B >> i) & 1
        s, carry = full_adder(a_bit, b_bit, carry)
        result |= (s << i)
    
    return result, carry

# Example: 5 + 8 = 13
# result, carry = ripple_carry_adder(5, 8)
# print(f"Result: {result}, Carry: {carry}")
examples.explanation: This code simulates a 4-bit ripple carry adder showing how carry propagates through each bit position sequentially. The full_adder function is called 4 times in sequence.

examples.title: Carry Look-Ahead Adder Implementation
examples.description: C program demonstrating parallel carry generation using generate and propagate
examples.explanation: This C implementation shows carry look-ahead logic where all carries are computed in parallel using generate (G) and propagate (P) signals rather than waiting for ripple. The carry equations are expanded to compute all carries simultaneously.

examples.title: ALU Control and Operation Selection
examples.description: ALU operation selection mechanism based on function code
examples.code:
CONSTANT ADD = 0b000
CONSTANT SUB = 0b001
CONSTANT AND_OP = 0b010
CONSTANT OR_OP = 0b011
CONSTANT XOR_OP = 0b100
CONSTANT NOT_OP = 0b101
CONSTANT INC = 0b110
CONSTANT DEC = 0b111

FUNCTION ALU(A, B, FunctionCode)
  RESULT = 0
  CARRY = 0
  
  SWITCH FunctionCode
    CASE ADD:
      RESULT = A + B          // Use adder circuit
      CARRY = (RESULT < A)   // Detect unsigned overflow
      
    CASE SUB:
      RESULT = A + (~B + 1)   // Two's complement subtraction
      CARRY = (RESULT > A)   // Borrow detection
      
    CASE AND_OP:
      RESULT = A AND B        // Bitwise AND
      
    CASE OR_OP:
      RESULT = A OR B         // Bitwise OR
      
    CASE XOR_OP:
      RESULT = A XOR B        // Bitwise XOR
      
    CASE NOT_OP:
      RESULT = NOT A          // Bitwise NOT (unary)
      
    CASE INC:
      RESULT = A + 1          // Increment
      
    CASE DEC:
      RESULT = A - 1         // Decrement
      
  END SWITCH
  
  // Update status flags
  ZERO = (RESULT == 0)
  SIGN = (RESULT < 0)         // MSB indicates sign
  OVERFLOW = (SUB and ((A > 0 and B < 0 and RESULT < 0) or 
                       (A < 0 and B > 0 and RESULT > 0)))
  
  RETURN (RESULT, CARRY, ZERO, SIGN, OVERFLOW)
END FUNCTION

// Control unit generates FunctionCode from instruction
FUNCTION ControlUnit(Instruction)
  OpCode = ExtractOpCode(Instruction)
  FunctionCode = Decode(OpCode)
  RETURN FunctionCode
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how ALU selects different operations based on function code, updates status flags, and how control unit determines which operation to perform.
